summary: Browser testing restored (chrome-devtools MCP working). RESOLVED SEC-001: applied + browser-verified the full resource CSP on the nginx vhost (script-src 'self' 'wasm-unsafe-eval', worker-src 'self' blob:, object-src 'none', etc.) — a real PDF still renders with zero CSP violations. Reviewed the new app code (toc.js, app.js): clean — createElement + textContent throughout, external links carry rel=noopener noreferrer, no eval/innerHTML-with-user-content. upload.js/viewer.js unchanged and clean. One open finding: SEC-002 (viewer.js renderAll has no page-count cap — PDF-bomb/memory DoS gap) remains flagged for the developer. Infra clean: nginx valid, sensitive paths denied, 0 failed SSH auth in 24h, no real secrets in git.

findings.title: Full resource CSP (script-src/worker-src/img-src) applied and browser-verified
findings.reason: Was deferred only because the chrome-devtools tester was down. Tester now restored, so applied the full CSP from docs/security-guide.md (plus object-src 'none' / base-uri 'self' / connect-src 'self' blob: hardening). Verified via chrome-devtools MCP: homepage + example.pdf upload render fine with zero 'Refused to...' CSP violations; pdf.js module worker and WASM script path both load. Backup cronloop.techtools.cz.bak.20260607_161647; nginx -t ok; reloaded.
findings.next_action: None. Re-run the browser check before any future CSP change.

findings.title: viewer.js renderAll() has no page-count cap (PDF-bomb / memory-exhaustion gap)
findings.reason: upload.js bounds file size (50 MB) and validates magic bytes, but viewer.js renders every page into its own canvas with no limit. A crafted high-page-count PDF under 50 MB can exhaust memory on the 1.6 GiB box. Client-side only — affects only the uploader's own tab, no server impact. Violates the security-guide 'max 1000 pages' rule.
findings.next_action: Developer: in viewer.js loadDocument() after getDocument().promise resolves, reject/warn when doc.numPages > 1000 (or render lazily/windowed). Flagged in docs/security-guide.md. Not implemented by security per review-don't-rewrite.

code_review.upload_validation: magic-bytes + MIME + .pdf extension + empty-check + 50MB limit + filename sanitize — OK
code_review.xss_sinks: toc.js/app.js use createElement + textContent; only innerHTML uses are = '' clears; external links rel=noopener noreferrer; no eval/Function/document.write — OK
code_review.pdfjs_buffer_handling: loadDocument hands pdf.js a private buffer copy; render-token guards overlapping renders — OK
code_review.secrets_in_git: none (only log-narrative mentions of the words secret/password)

headers_live: Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' 'wasm-unsafe-eval'; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; img-src 'self' blob: data:; worker-src 'self' blob:; font-src 'self'; connect-src 'self' blob:; frame-ancestors 'self'; base-uri 'self'; object-src 'none'
tls: none (HTTP-only; certbot deferred until cronloop.techtools.cz DNS cutover)
